era_context: The High Middle Ages; the investiture controversy between the papacy and the Holy Roman Empire continues. In Iberia, the Reconquista is advancing with El Cid's campaigns. The Byzantine Empire is stabilizing under Alexios I Komnenos. In East Asia, the Song Dynasty rules China; Japan is in the late Heian period. The Islamic world is experiencing political fragmentation, particularly in the Seljuk Empire.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Central Asia beyond Islamic sources (limited documentation)

events.title: El Cid besieges Valencia; campaign against Almoravids
events.description: Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, known as El Cid, begins or continues his siege of Valencia, which is held by Almoravid forces. The siege is part of El Cid's broader campaign to expand Christian territory in eastern Iberia and to establish himself as an independent military power. The siege will last several years and culminate in the conquest of Valencia in 1094. El Cid's campaigns represent a significant phase of the Reconquista and demonstrate the fragmentation of Almoravid power in Iberia.
key_figures: Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, El Cid (military commander)

sources.name: Poema de Mio Cid (12th-century epic poem, based on oral tradition and later compilation)
Chronicle of Alfonso VI (near-contemporary Castilian chronicle)
Historiographic consensus on El Cid's campaigns

events.certainty_note: The siege of Valencia is attested in near-contemporary chronicles, but the exact chronology is uncertain. The Poema de Mio Cid is a later literary work, not a contemporary source. The siege likely began in the late 1080s or early 1090s and lasted until 1094.
cross_references: 1086-001 (Battle of Sagrajas, earlier Almoravid intervention in Iberia)

events.title: William II Rufus consolidates Norman rule in England
events.description: King William II Rufus of England continues to consolidate Norman rule and strengthen royal authority. In 1090, William II is engaged in conflicts with rebellious Norman barons and is working to expand royal power at the expense of the nobility. His reign is marked by conflicts with the church (particularly over investiture) and by military campaigns in Wales and Scotland. William II's rule represents the continuation of Norman consolidation in England and the strengthening of the English monarchy.
events.certainty_note: William II's reign and his consolidation of power are well-attested in contemporary and near-contemporary English chronicles. The specific events of 1090 are less clearly documented than the broader pattern of his rule.

events.title: Alexios I Komnenos strengthens Byzantine military position
events.description: Emperor Alexios I Komnenos continues his efforts to stabilize and strengthen the Byzantine Empire after the catastrophic loss of Anatolia to the Seljuks following the Battle of Manzikert (1071). In 1090, Alexios is engaged in military campaigns to recover lost territories and to strengthen the empire's defenses. His efforts include the recruitment of mercenaries, the reorganization of the military, and diplomatic negotiations with neighboring powers. These efforts lay the groundwork for the later Byzantine recovery and the call for Western military aid that will lead to the First Crusade.

sources.name: Alexiad by Anna Komnenos (near-contemporary Byzantine chronicle, written early 12th century)
Historiographic consensus on Alexios's military campaigns

events.certainty_note: Alexios's military campaigns and efforts to strengthen the Byzantine Empire are well-attested in the Alexiad and scholarly consensus. The specific events of 1090 are part of a broader pattern of military activity rather than a discrete event.
cross_references: 1071-001 (Battle of Manzikert, context for Alexios's efforts)
1095-002 (First Crusade call, consequence of Byzantine military weakness)

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of El Cid's siege of Valencia is uncertain; the siege likely began in the late 1080s or early 1090s, but the exact chronology is disputed. The Poema de Mio Cid, while culturally significant, is a 12th-century literary work and not a reliable historical source for precise chronology. The significance of William II's consolidation of power in 1090 is difficult to assess from surviving sources; the Anglo-Saxon Chronicle provides limited detail for this specific year. Alexios's military campaigns are attested in the Alexiad, but the specific events of 1090 are not clearly distinguished from the broader pattern of his reign.
historiographic_note: The year 1090 is moderately well-documented in Western European sources (English and Iberian chronicles) but less well-attested in Byzantine and Islamic sources. The historical record is heavily weighted toward political and military events; economic, social, and cultural developments are underrepresented. The surviving records reflect the biases of literate elites (clergy, nobility) and omit the experiences of peasants, merchants, and non-elite populations. Islamic sources for this period are fragmentary and often focus on dynastic politics rather than specific years.

graph_edges.note: El Cid's siege of Valencia in 1090 led to the conquest of the city in 1094
William II's consolidation of power continued until his death in 1100
Alexios's military weakness prompted the Byzantine request for Western military aid
